title: Blue Ridge Labs Founder Fellowship 2026: Turn Bold Social Impact Ideas Into Fundable Ventures
summary: Innovative ideas have the power to reshape societies, tackle inequality, and improve economic mobility. However, transforming a promising concept into a sustainable venture often requires the right combination of mentorship, funding, research support, and access to a strong professional network. To support emerging entrepreneurs who want to build impactful solutions, Blue Ridge Labs , the venture studio of Robin Hood Foundation , has opened applications for the 2026 Founder Fellowship . This program is designed to help early-stage founders transform bold ideas into minimum viable products (MVPs) and fundable ventures capable of addressing pressing social challenges. The fellowship is structured to guide participants through the process of testing ideas, validating solutions with real users, and building technology-driven ventures that promote economic mobility and social change. About Blue Ridge Labs Blue Ridge Labs is a venture studio created by the Robin Hood Foundation , a major philanthropic organization focused on fighting poverty and increasing economic opportunity. Through its programs and venture-building initiatives, Blue Ridge Labs supports entrepreneurs working on innovative solutions to systemic social issues. The Founder Fellowship is one of its flagship initiatives, helping mission-driven founders transform early ideas into scalable ventures that can attract funding and deliver meaningful impact. A Fellowship Designed for Early-Stage Innovators The Founder Fellowship specifically targets individuals who are passionate about solving social problems but may not yet have a fully developed startup. Unlike many startup accelerators that require a prototype or existing product, this fellowship welcomes applicants at the idea stage . Participants only need a clear concept, a strong motivation to build, and the determination to transform their idea into a working venture. The fellowship helps founders move through the critical early stages of entrepreneurship, including research, validation, product development, and initial fundraising preparation. What Participants Will Gain The Blue Ridge Labs Founder Fellowship provides an extensive range of resources designed to support founders during the earliest stages of venture creation. Weekly Learning and Development Sessions Participants join structured weekly sessions led by experienced founders, entrepreneurs, and industry professionals. These sessions cover key areas essential for startup success, including: Customer research and problem validation Product development strategies Startup growth and traction building Fundraising preparation and investor engagement These sessions ensure that fellows gain practical knowledge and insights while actively building their ventures. Direct User Feedback and Market Insights A crucial step in building a successful product is understanding the needs of real users. Through the fellowship, participants gain access to the Design Insights Group (DIG) community, which provides valuable feedback and research insights. This allows founders to test their ideas, refine their concepts, and ensure that the solutions they build address real-world needs. Personalized Mentorship and Expert Guidance Fellows benefit from continuous support through: One-on-one mentorship Group coaching sessions Hands-on technical assistance Experts in engineering, product design, and entrepreneurship provide guidance throughout the program, helping founders overcome technical and strategic challenges. $20,000 Stipend to Build Your Venture One of the most valuable aspects of the fellowship is the $20,000 stipend provided to each participant. This financial support allows founders to dedicate focused time to developing their ideas, conducting research, and building their MVP without immediate financial pressure. Collaborative Workspace in Brooklyn During the in-person stage of the program, fellows work alongside other entrepreneurs at the Blue Ridge Labs workspace in Brooklyn , New York City . This collaborative environment encourages creativity, peer learning, and partnerships among founders who share a commitment to building solutions for social impact. Access to a Powerful Network Fellows will also have the opportunity to showcase their ventures to leaders across multiple sectors, including: Technology Philanthropy Public policy Through the network of the Robin Hood Foundation , participants gain exposure to influential stakeholders who can help accelerate their ventures. Program Structure: A 20-Week Journey From Idea to Venture The Founder Fellowship follows a structured roadmap designed to guide participants through the early stages of venture development. Step 1: Explore and Test (Virtual Phase) The program begins with a remote phase where fellows explore their ideas, conduct research, and test their assumptions with potential users. During this stage, participants focus on validating their concept and identifying the real needs of their target audience. Step 2: Build Your MVP (In-Person Phase) In the second stage, fellows move into an in-person environment at the Blue Ridge Labs space in Brooklyn . Here, participants work intensively to build their minimum viable product and prepare their venture for potential investment. By the end of the 20-week fellowship, founders will have transformed their concept into a validated product prototype and a venture ready for further development or funding. Who Should Apply The fellowship is ideal for individuals who: Have an idea for a social impact technology venture Want to address challenges related to economic mobility and social equity Are passionate about turning ideas into real solutions Are ready to commit to a structured venture-building program Applicants do not need a prototype, technical background, or co-founder to apply. The fellowship is designed to help founders move from idea to MVP through structured support. Why This Fellowship Matters Many of the world’s most transformative startups began as simple ideas driven by individuals determined to solve real problems. However, without access to mentorship, funding, and networks, promising ideas often struggle to reach their full potential. The Blue Ridge Labs Founder Fellowship bridges this gap by providing the resources, community, and financial support that early-stage founders need to build ventures capable of creating lasting social impact. For entrepreneurs passionate about addressing inequality and improving economic opportunity, this fellowship represents a powerful opportunity to turn a bold idea into a scalable solution. Deadline The application deadline is May 3, 2026 . Applicants interested in joining the 2026 cohort , which begins in June 2026 , are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline. For more information click here Apply here Discover more opportunities here The post Blue Ridge Labs Founder Fellowship 2026: Turn Bold Social Impact Ideas Into Fundable Ventures appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Powerful Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 for Graduate Students in Washington DC
summary: The Powerful Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates interested in international affairs, human rights, and security policy to gain practical experience working at a U.S. government commission in Washington, DC. This highly respected fellowship program prepares emerging leaders to engage with complex global challenges while contributing to policy discussions related to human rights, security cooperation, and economic collaboration. The fellowship is hosted by the U.S. Commission on Security and Cooperation in Europe, also known as the Helsinki Commission. Named after longtime U.S. Ambassador Max Kampelman, the fellowship recognizes and develops the next generation of leaders working in security policy, diplomacy, and strategic communications. The commission plays a vital role in advancing national security by promoting cooperation and stability among 57 countries across Europe, Eurasia, and North America through initiatives related to human rights, economic cooperation, and military security. Applications for the Summer 2026 Max Kampelman Fellowship are currently open and will close on March 29, 2026. Max Kampelman Fellowship Overview and Work Experience The Max Kampelman Fellowship provides participants with the opportunity to work alongside experienced policymakers, researchers, and communications specialists at the Helsinki Commission. Fellows contribute directly to policy discussions and research activities that influence international security cooperation and human rights policy. The fellowship lasts three months, with fellows working approximately 30 hours per week in person at the commission’s office in Washington, DC. Fellows receive $27 per hour for their work, along with access to professional development opportunities, mentorship from senior commission staff, and networking events with policy professionals. During their fellowship period, participants gain firsthand experience observing how U.S. congressional commissions operate and how international policy initiatives are developed and implemented. Max Kampelman Policy Fellowship Opportunities Participants applying for the Policy Fellowship track will work closely with commission policy advisors focusing on issues related to political and military affairs, economic and environmental matters, and human rights and fundamental freedoms. Policy fellows contribute to research and policy analysis covering developments throughout the Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) region, which includes the 57 participating countries across Europe, Eurasia, and North America. Typical responsibilities for policy fellows include researching emerging political, economic, and human rights trends, assisting with congressional hearings and briefings, supporting legislative initiatives, and contributing to policy publications. Fellows may also attend congressional hearings, policy events, and international briefings relevant to the commission’s work. Each policy fellow is expected to produce at least one research article or policy piece during the fellowship period that may be published on the commission’s website. Max Kampelman Communications Fellowship Opportunities The Communications Fellowship track focuses on public engagement, media outreach, and strategic communications related to the commission’s work. Communications fellows support various projects that help raise public awareness of the commission’s initiatives. Fellows assist with promoting hearings and briefings, managing media outreach, developing digital content, and supporting social media communications. Responsibilities may also include drafting press materials, preparing content for the commission’s website, assisting with event communications, and supporting multimedia storytelling initiatives. Like policy fellows, communications fellows are also expected to produce one article or content piece during their fellowship that may be published on the commission’s website. Max Kampelman Professional Development and Networking One of the most valuable aspects of the Max Kampelman Fellowship is the opportunity to work within a professional policy environment in Washington, DC. Fellows gain exposure to high-level discussions on international security, human rights, and diplomacy. The program also provides mentorship from experienced policy advisors and communications professionals who guide fellows throughout the fellowship period. These mentorship opportunities help fellows strengthen their research, writing, and policy analysis skills while building valuable professional networks. Fellows are encouraged to attend policy events, panel discussions, congressional hearings, and other public forums that provide deeper insight into international policy issues. This immersive experience provides participants with the practical knowledge and professional connections needed to pursue careers in government, international organizations, research institutions, and policy advocacy. Max Kampelman Application Process Applicants interested in the Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 must submit a complete application package by email. The application package should include a brief cover letter explaining the applicant’s interest in working for the commission and describing relevant academic or professional experiences. Applicants should also indicate their availability, including preferred start and end dates and expected weekly working hours. Applicants must also submit a resume limited to two pages and a writing sample of no more than three pages. Communications fellowship applicants may optionally include samples of creative work such as copywriting, graphic design, photography, or multimedia projects. Applications must be sent to the official fellowship email address provided by the commission. Applicants should include their first initial and last name in the email subject line, along with the fellowship track they are applying for. Only complete applications submitted before the official deadline will be considered. Deadline for Applications The deadline to apply for the Summer 2026 Max Kampelman Fellowship is March 29, 2026. Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to ensure their materials are reviewed. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Powerful Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 and apply for the program, visit the official application page here . Conclusion The Max Kampelman Fellowship 2026 provides an exceptional opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates interested in international policy, security affairs, and human rights to gain meaningful professional experience within a U.S. government commission. By working alongside policy experts and communications professionals, fellows gain valuable insights into international diplomacy, congressional policy processes, and global security cooperation. Participants leave the program with stronger research and policy skills, expanded professional networks, and practical experience that can launch successful careers in international affairs and public policy. title: Empowering Young Entrepreneurs in Kenya: Apply for the GIZ Accelerator Programme for Youth-Led Businesses
summary: Young entrepreneurs play a crucial role in driving innovation, economic growth, and job creation. Across Kenya, youth-led enterprises are transforming local industries by introducing creative solutions, strengthening value chains, and building sustainable businesses that benefit their communities. However, many early-stage businesses face common challenges, including limited access to finance, inadequate management training, and insufficient professional networks. To address these barriers and support the next generation of business leaders, the German development agency Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) in Kenya, in collaboration with Kenya Institute of Business Training and the Youth Enterprise Development Fund , has launched a dedicated accelerator programme designed to empower youth entrepreneurs. This initiative focuses on strengthening business management capabilities, improving financial access, and creating opportunities for sustainable growth among young innovators operating in key sectors of Kenya’s economy. Supporting Youth-Led Businesses in Key Kenyan Counties This accelerator programme specifically targets entrepreneurs operating in Kiambu County , Machakos County , Kajiado County , and Nairobi County . These regions represent some of the most vibrant entrepreneurial hubs in Kenya, where young people are establishing businesses that contribute to both local and national economic development. Entrepreneurs selected for the programme will receive comprehensive support aimed at helping them scale their operations, improve operational efficiency, and strengthen their competitive advantage in the market. Focus Sectors: Strengthening High-Impact Industries The accelerator programme is designed to support businesses operating within sectors that have strong potential for economic transformation and employment creation. Eligible enterprises must be involved in one of the following areas: Agro-Processing and Food Value Addition Agro-processing plays a critical role in enhancing agricultural productivity and reducing post-harvest losses. Businesses working in food value addition—such as packaging, preservation, processing, or transforming raw agricultural products into higher-value goods—are encouraged to apply. By strengthening these enterprises, the programme aims to improve food systems and create new market opportunities for farmers and processors. Leather Processing and Value Addition Kenya possesses a strong livestock sector, creating significant potential for the leather industry. Businesses focused on leather processing, product development, and value addition—such as leather goods manufacturing—are vital for strengthening domestic production and reducing reliance on imported finished products. Textile Processing and Value Addition The textile sector continues to offer strong opportunities for youth entrepreneurship, particularly in design, production, and value-added manufacturing. Enterprises that process textiles or develop finished textile products contribute to local industry growth and promote job creation. Programme Objectives: Accelerating Sustainable Growth The accelerator programme aims to empower young entrepreneurs through a structured approach that addresses several key areas essential for business success. Strengthening Business Management Skills Participants will gain access to targeted training and mentorship designed to enhance their leadership and business management capabilities. The programme helps entrepreneurs develop strategic planning skills, improve operational systems, and adopt sustainable growth strategies. Expanding Professional Networks and Linkages Entrepreneurial success often depends on access to strong professional networks. Through this programme, participants will have opportunities to connect with industry experts, mentors, financial institutions, and fellow entrepreneurs. These connections can lead to valuable partnerships and collaborative opportunities. Improving Access to Finance Many early-stage businesses struggle to secure funding for expansion. The accelerator programme helps entrepreneurs better understand financing options, prepare investment-ready proposals, and connect with financial institutions that support youth-led enterprises. Accelerating Business Growth Ultimately, the goal of the programme is to help participants scale their businesses sustainably. By combining training, mentorship, and networking opportunities, the initiative provides the tools and resources needed to help entrepreneurs grow their enterprises and strengthen their market presence. Why This Opportunity Matters Entrepreneurship among young people is one of the most powerful drivers of economic transformation in Africa. By investing in youth-led businesses, programmes like this accelerator contribute to: Job creation and economic inclusion Strengthening local value chains Promoting innovation and sustainable industries Supporting long-term economic resilience Young entrepreneurs often possess the creativity, energy, and vision needed to transform traditional industries. With the right support systems in place, their businesses can evolve into powerful engines of economic growth. Who Should Apply The programme is designed for entrepreneurs who are ready to grow their businesses and take them to the next level. Applicants should: Be between 18 and 35 years old Operate a business that is less than seven years old Run enterprises located in Kiambu, Machakos, Kajiado, or Nairobi counties Operate in agro-processing, leather processing, or textile value addition sectors Entrepreneurs who are passionate about innovation, sustainability, and business growth are particularly encouraged to apply. How This Accelerator Can Transform Your Business Participating in an accelerator programme can be a turning point for many businesses. Beyond training and mentorship, accelerators provide exposure, credibility, and opportunities that can significantly accelerate growth. For young entrepreneurs in Kenya, this programme represents a chance to gain the knowledge, networks, and financial insights necessary to strengthen their enterprises and compete more effectively in the market. If you are a young business owner seeking to scale your enterprise and expand your impact, this accelerator programme offers a unique opportunity to gain the tools and support needed for long-term success. Deadline No specific application deadline has been announced yet. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as soon as possible once the application portal is open. Apply here Discover more opportunities here The post Empowering Young Entrepreneurs in Kenya: Apply for the GIZ Accelerator Programme for Youth-Led Businesses appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge 2026: Win €2,000 Funding, Mentorship, or Tech Support for Climate-Smart Agribusiness Ideas
summary: The Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge 2026 (Window 2) is a powerful opportunity for young and emerging agripreneurs to transform their ideas into viable, climate-smart businesses. Designed and implemented by the Agri-Business Facility for Africa (ABF) in collaboration with GIZ and international partners, this initiative provides a free, structured learning pathway that equips participants with the skills, tools, and knowledge required to build investment-ready agribusiness concepts. In addition to gaining high-quality training, participants stand a chance to win €2,000 in funding , mentorship support , or a tablet/laptop , making this program a unique blend of learning, innovation, and opportunity. About the Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge The Agribusiness e-Academy is designed to address a critical gap in Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem: limited access to funding for early-stage agribusinesses . Statistics show that only a small percentage of startups secure external funding due to weak business models, lack of financial clarity, and insufficient climate-smart planning. This initiative directly tackles these challenges by offering: A step-by-step online learning pathway Practical tools for building investment-ready agribusinesses Exposure to climate-smart agricultural practices Opportunities to pitch and win funding or support Why This Opportunity Matters The Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge is designed to help participants: Build strong business logic and financial viability Develop climate-resilient agribusiness strategies Increase their chances of securing investment Transform ideas into scalable and sustainable enterprises This is particularly important in Africa, where agribusiness plays a key role in economic development, employment, and food security. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be 18 years or older Be based in a member state of the Organisation of African, Caribbean and Pacific States (OACPS) Have an agribusiness idea related to: Cocoa Maize Cashew Livestock (cattle, sheep, goats) This ensures that the program targets high-impact agricultural value chains. Learning Pathway: Step-by-Step Breakdown Participants must complete a structured learning journey consisting of the following courses: 1. Business Model Canvas for Agribusinesses Learn how to design and structure a business model Understand costs, pricing, and revenue streams 2. Climate-Smart Agribusiness: Adaptation Identify climate risks within your value chain Develop risk mitigation and adaptation strategies 3. Creating an Investment-Ready Agribusiness Assess business viability Prepare your idea for investors and funding opportunities 4. Develop Your AgriCanvas Apply all learning into a Business Model Canvas tailored to your idea Transform your concept into an investment-ready proposal ACCESS ALL YOUR COURSES HERE What You Can Win Participants who successfully complete the program and submit their AgriCanvas stand a chance to win: €2,000 in funding Mentorship support from experts A tablet or laptop These rewards are designed to support the growth and implementation of innovative agribusiness ideas. How to Apply: Step-by-Step Guide Follow these steps carefully to submit a valid application: Step 1: Register on atingi Create a free account on the atingi platform Access all required courses Step 2: Complete Mandatory Courses Business Model Canvas for Agribusinesses Climate-Smart Agribusiness: Adaptation Creating an Investment-Ready Agribusiness Note: The third course unlocks only after completing the first two. Step 3: Complete Your AgriCanvas Use the official Business Model Canvas template Clearly structure your agribusiness idea Step 4: Submit Your Idea Fill out the Official Submission Form Enter your AgriCanvas directly (no uploads allowed) Provide your certificate ID as proof of course completion Important Submission Requirements To ensure your application is considered: Only submissions via the official form will be reviewed A valid certificate ID is mandatory AgriCanvas must be entered directly into the form Only one submission per applicant is allowed Application Deadline All submissions must be completed by: 31 May 2026 Late or incomplete submissions will not be considered. Selection and Evaluation Process Applications will be assessed based on: Business viability and clarity Climate-smart innovation Financial sustainability Impact potential Alignment with eligible value chains Winners will be announced around September 2026 and contacted via email. Learn more about the Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge 2026 HERE. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Who can enter this challenge? Young and emerging agripreneurs aged 18+ from OACPS member states. Do I need to pay to participate? No. The program and all courses are completely free. Can I submit more than one idea? No. Only one submission per applicant is allowed per window. Do I need to complete all courses? Yes. All three mandatory courses must be completed to qualify. What happens if I am not selected? You can improve your idea and reapply in future submission windows. Can I participate if I have taken other courses? Yes, but you must complete the required three courses. How will winners be selected? An expert committee will evaluate submissions based on predefined criteria. What if my idea is outside the listed value chains? It will not be considered. Only cocoa, maize, cashew, and livestock ideas are eligible. How will I know if I am selected? Winners will be contacted via email linked to their atingi account. Click Here to Download the Terms Conditions Document for Application Discover other global Grant and Competition opportunities on OFY. The post Agribusiness e-Academy Innovation Challenge 2026: Win €2,000 Funding, Mentorship, or Tech Support for Climate-Smart Agribusiness Ideas appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 🌍 Future Leaders Programme 14 at SOAS: Industrial Policy for Structural Transformation in the Global South (Apply by 20 April 2026)
summary: The Future Leaders Programme (FLP 14) , hosted by the Development Leadership Dialogue (DLD) at SOAS University of London, is a prestigious, high-level leadership workshop designed for experienced professionals shaping the future of economic development in the Global South. This intensive programme brings together emerging leaders from across sectors to explore industrial policy, structural transformation, and sustainable development strategies in a rapidly evolving global landscape. 📌 Programme Overview The Future Leaders Programme is a flagship initiative of the Development Leadership Dialogue, co-directed by leading development economists including Ha-Joon Chang, Christopher Cramer, and Jonathan Di John. The programme aims to bridge gaps between key development actors—governments, private sector, civil society, academia, and international organizations—through dialogue, collaboration, and shared learning. Participants will engage in: Expert-led lectures Interactive discussions and debates Real-world case studies Peer-to-peer learning with global professionals 📅 Key Details Programme Dates: 22–26 June 2026 Location: London, United Kingdom Application Deadline: 20 April 2026 Programme Fee: £3,000 Scholarships: Limited full and partial funding available 🎯 Focus Area: Industrial Policy in the Global South This year’s theme— Industrial Policy for Structural Transformation in the Global South —addresses one of the most critical challenges facing developing economies today. Participants will explore: 1. Economic Transformation Challenges Premature deindustrialization Over-reliance on commodity exports Low-productivity service sector growth 2. Climate and Sustainability Pressures Balancing development with climate responsibility Leveraging renewable energy opportunities Addressing ecological degradation 3. Technology and Innovation Digital transformation and AI Technology transfer and global inequality Investment in R D and skills development 4. Global Economic Shifts Changing geopolitical dynamics South-South cooperation Evolving trade and policy environments 🧠 What You Will Gain Participating in FLP 14 offers: Direct engagement with globally recognized experts and policymakers Deep insights into industrial policy design and implementation Exposure to real-world development challenges and solutions A powerful network of high-level professionals across sectors Practical tools for influencing policy and driving economic transformation 🎤 Confirmed Speakers The programme features distinguished speakers from top institutions and policy backgrounds, including: Antonio Andreoni (SOAS) José Miguel Ahumada (University of Chile) Andres Arauz (Former Minister, Ecuador) Yemi Dipeolu (Former Economic Advisor, Nigeria) Isabel Estevez (Mexico) Jostein Hauge (University of Cambridge) Verena Hitner (Brazil Ministry of Science) Amir Lebdioui (University of Oxford) Pamela Mondliwa (South Africa) Richard Kozul-Wright (SOAS, former UNCTAD Chief Economist) João Silva (University of Lisbon) Yin Shao Loong (Malaysia) 📝 Application Process Applicants must complete an online application form and provide: A personal statement outlining their views on industrial policy challenges in the Global South Evidence of relevant professional experience Demonstration of leadership potential and future impact Applicants seeking financial support must: Clearly explain their financial situation Specify the amount of funding required APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more short courses opportunities, click here . ⚠️ Important Considerations This is a highly selective and intensive programme with a small cohort. Applicants should carefully consider: The total cost (programme fee + travel and accommodation) The competitive selection process The advanced level of discussion and participation required 🚀 Final Thoughts The Future Leaders Programme 14 is a unique opportunity for experienced professionals to deepen their understanding of industrial policy while building a global network of changemakers. If you are committed to shaping economic transformation in the Global South and ready to engage at a high level, this programme could be a powerful step forward in your leadership journey. title: SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue 2026: A Powerful Global Opportunity for Women-Led Businesses in the Caribbean
summary: Women entrepreneurs across the Caribbean now have a major opportunity to gain international visibility, connect with buyers, and attract investment through the SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue 2026. This initiative, led by the Caribbean Development Bank (CDB) in collaboration with the International Trade Centre (ITC), is a strategic platform designed to elevate women-led businesses (WLBs) into regional and global markets. What Is the SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue? The SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue is a regional showcase platform that highlights selected women-led businesses to a wide network of: International buyers Investors Trade partners Regional distributors Global value chain actors This catalogue is not just a directory—it is a strategic marketing and visibility tool designed to position Caribbean women entrepreneurs as competitive players in international trade. By being featured, businesses gain a unique opportunity to: Increase brand exposure across global markets Attract partnerships and investment opportunities Build credibility through association with recognized institutions Expand export potential Why This Opportunity Matters Women-led businesses often face barriers such as limited access to markets, financing, and global networks. This initiative directly addresses those challenges by: Providing international visibility Enhancing market access opportunities Supporting inclusive trade development Strengthening regional economic participation of women entrepreneurs The involvement of globally recognized organizations like the International Trade Centre ensures that selected businesses are positioned within a credible and influential trade ecosystem. Who Can Apply? This opportunity is specifically designed for: Eligible Applicants: Women-led businesses (WLBs) Entrepreneurs operating within the Caribbean region Businesses formally registered and operational Geographic Eligibility: Applicants must be based in Caribbean countries. While the exact country list is not specified, it typically includes nations within the Caribbean Community (CARICOM) and surrounding island states. Mandatory Eligibility Requirement To be considered for inclusion, applicants must: Be a registered member of the SheTrades Caribbean Hub If you are not yet registered, you must complete your membership registration before applying. Application Requirements Applicants are required to provide the following information: Legal Company Name Country of Registration Website or Social Media Handle (to be published in the catalogue) Primary Contact Email (will be publicly listed) Primary Contact Phone Number (will be publicly listed) Confirmation of SheTrades Caribbean Hub Membership All submitted details will be used to build your business profile within the catalogue. Apply here Data Privacy and Confidentiality Both the Caribbean Development Bank and the International Trade Centre emphasize strict confidentiality in handling applicant data. Information provided is used solely for analytical and catalogue development purposes Sensitive business data is protected and not misused Benefits of Being Selected Being included in the SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue offers multiple strategic advantages: 1. Global Exposure Your business will be visible to international stakeholders actively seeking partnerships. 2. Increased Credibility Association with reputable institutions boosts trust and brand reputation. 3. Market Expansion Access new markets beyond your local or regional reach. 4. Networking Opportunities Connect with buyers, investors, and trade facilitators. 5. Business Growth Potential Unlock opportunities for scaling operations and increasing revenue streams. Deadline 30th March 2026 The SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue 2026 is more than just a listing—it is a gateway to global business growth, visibility, and opportunity for women entrepreneurs in the Caribbean. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post SheTrades Caribbean Business Catalogue 2026: A Powerful Global Opportunity for Women-Led Businesses in the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge 2026: Grants, Incubation & Mentorship for Women-Led Enterprises in India
summary: The Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge 2026 is a transformative initiative designed to reshape the childcare ecosystem in urban India. Led by UN Women India with support from the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in India and implemented by Primus Partners Pvt. Ltd., this program seeks to identify, support, and scale innovative childcare solutions that empower women and strengthen urban care systems. Childcare remains one of the most critical barriers to women s economic participation, particularly in low-income urban communities. This challenge directly addresses issues of accessibility, affordability, quality, and sustainability in childcare services by supporting enterprises that deliver innovative and scalable solutions. About the Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge The Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge is a structured incubation program that focuses on: Supporting women-led and women-benefiting childcare enterprises Promoting sustainable and scalable business models Strengthening urban care systems Reducing unpaid care burdens on women Advancing women’s economic empowerment Selected enterprises will undergo a 3-month incubation and mentorship program , gaining access to funding, networks, and technical expertise. Objectives of the Initiative The program is designed with clear, impact-driven objectives: Identify and support 8–10 high-potential childcare enterprises through a competitive selection process. Provide tailored incubation, mentorship, and capacity-building support . Offer technical guidance and financial assistance to build investor-ready models. Strengthen linkages with policymakers, investors, and ecosystem stakeholders . Enable long-term sustainability and scalability of childcare solutions . What Makes This Challenge Unique? (Innovation Focus Areas) The initiative emphasizes innovation across multiple dimensions: 1. Delivery and Scale Innovation Community-based childcare models Workplace-linked childcare solutions Mobile and flexible-hour services Hybrid delivery systems 2. Financing and Business Model Innovation Subscription-based childcare models Employer-supported childcare systems Public-private partnership models Voucher and cross-subsidized systems 3. Systems Integration Alignment with public schemes and policies Integration with employer ecosystems Collaboration with local urban bodies 4. Quality Assurance and Workforce Development Training and certification for childcare workers Standardized safety and quality systems Workforce retention strategies 5. Digital and Sustainability Innovation Use of data systems and digital tools Monitoring dashboards Climate-resilient childcare solutions Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge, applicants must: Be women-led or women-benefiting enterprises Operate within urban or peri-urban areas in India Demonstrate a proof of concept (pilot or early-stage validation) Address childcare needs of low-income or informal sector workers Show strong potential for scalability and replication Align with public or private systems integration opportunities Program Benefits Selected participants will gain access to a wide range of benefits: 1. Strategic Positioning Clear direction for scaling and growth Stronger market positioning 2. Operational Strengthening Improved service delivery systems Compliance and quality enhancement 3. Partnership Development Connections with public, private, and community stakeholders 4. Financial Readiness Investment preparedness Financial sustainability planning 5. Gender-Responsive Business Models Integration of gender equality principles into operations 6. Mentorship Guidance from industry experts and experienced entrepreneurs 7. Capacity Development Support from specialists in gender and care economy 8. Grant Support Selected enterprises may receive grants of up to USD 5,000 Program Timeline The Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge follows a structured journey: 16 March 2026 – Applications Open 16 April 2026 – Application Deadline 20 April 2026 – Shortlisting and Cohort Announcement 30 April 2026 – Launch National Roundtable 1 May – 30 July 2026 – Incubation Mentorship Program 5 August 2026 – Demo Day Grand Finale Program Journey Explained Participants will go through a carefully designed process: Application and selection Onboarding and orientation Intensive incubation and mentorship Networking and stakeholder engagement Final pitch during Demo Day Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) What is the Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge? It is an incubation program designed to support innovative childcare enterprises that improve access and quality of childcare services in urban India. Who can apply for this program? Women-led or women-benefiting enterprises working on childcare solutions in urban or peri-urban India. Are early-stage startups eligible? Yes, as long as they have a validated proof of concept or pilot . Is financial support provided? Yes, selected enterprises may receive grants of up to USD 5,000 . What type of support will participants receive? Participants will receive: Mentorship Capacity building Technical assistance Networking opportunities Investment readiness support How many enterprises will be selected? Approximately 8 to 10 enterprises will be selected. What is the duration of the program? The incubation program runs for approximately 3 months . How can I apply? Applications are submitted through the Official UN Platform during the application window. Click HERE to learn more about the Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge 2026. Final Thoughts The Urban Childcare Innovation Challenge 2026 represents a powerful opportunity for women-led enterprises to transform childcare systems while scaling sustainable business solutions. By bridging innovation, gender equality, and economic empowerment, this initiative is set to redefine urban childcare across India. Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY HERE. title: 🌍 INTERPOL Communications Officer Job Opportunity (Reserve List 2026): Global Career in Strategic Communications
summary: If you re a communications professional looking to make a global impact, this opportunity with INTERPOL could be your next big career move. As the world’s largest international police organization, INTERPOL plays a critical role in combating international crime—and now, they are seeking talented individuals to join their Communications Directorate through a reserve list for Communications Officers. This role offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of global security, strategic messaging, and international collaboration. 📌 About the Role The Communications Officer (Reserve List – Vacancy Notice 1710) will provide strategic communications support across various INTERPOL projects and departments. Successful candidates may be placed in multiple duty stations worldwide and will work closely with the Campaigns Advocacy team. This is not just a communications role—it’s an opportunity to shape narratives, influence global audiences, and support international law enforcement efforts. 🗓️ Application Deadline 30 March 2026 📍 Location Multiple Duty Stations (Global) 🧾 Contract Details Contract Type: Fixed-term and Short-term Duration: Up to 3 years (with possible extensions based on performance and funding) Grade: 5 Security Screening: Basic 💼 Key Responsibilities 1. Strategic Communications Advisory Develop and implement communication strategies and campaigns Advise stakeholders on messaging, engagement, and communication channels Monitor and evaluate communication impact 2. Content Creation Across Platforms Produce high-quality content: press releases, blogs, social media posts, scripts Develop visual content such as videos and infographics Ensure consistent branding and messaging 3. Stakeholder Engagement Build partnerships with governments, NGOs, media, and international organizations Manage media relations and external communications Identify collaboration opportunities 4. Storytelling Case Studies Create compelling narratives and human-interest stories Highlight project impact through case studies Promote success stories internally and externally 5. Additional Contributions Participate in field missions, conferences, and training Support donor engagement and funding communications Stay updated on communication trends and law enforcement developments 🎓 Requirements Education Bachelor’s degree (3–4 years) in a relevant field Master’s degree is an advantage OR 10 years of relevant experience if education criteria not met Experience Minimum 3 years in communications (international or government settings preferred) Proven experience in campaigns, advocacy, and strategy Strong media and stakeholder engagement skills Familiarity with digital tools, CMS, and social media Languages Fluency in English Knowledge of Arabic, French, or Spanish is a plus Skills Excellent writing and presentation skills Strategic thinking and creativity Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment Strong cross-cultural collaboration skills 🌟 Why You Should Apply Working with INTERPOL means contributing to global safety and security while advancing your professional career in an international environment. You’ll collaborate with experts across countries, influence global narratives, and help shape impactful communication strategies. 📢 Recruitment Process Notes Interviews/tests expected ~6 weeks after deadline Selected candidates typically start within 2–3 months This process will create a reserve list for future similar roles Applications are merit-based 🌍 Diversity Inclusion INTERPOL strongly encourages applications from: Women Nationals from underrepresented member countries 🔗 How to Apply Visit the official INTERPOL careers page and search for Vacancy Notice 1710 – Communications Officer (Reserve List) . title: Prestigious OPEC Fund Internship 2026 for Global Students
summary: The Prestigious OPEC Fund Internship 2026 offers an excellent opportunity for students and recent graduates from around the world to gain practical experience within an international development organization. Hosted by The OPEC Fund for International Development, this internship program allows young professionals to work in a dynamic and multicultural environment while contributing to development initiatives that impact countries globally. The internship program typically runs between three and six months and is designed to provide participants with hands-on experience in various departments within the organization. Interns will gain exposure to real-world development finance operations and build valuable professional networks in the international development sector. The application deadline is April 12, 2026, and all nationalities are eligible to apply. However, candidates from OPEC Fund member countries are strongly encouraged to submit their applications. This internship represents a valuable opportunity for individuals interested in development finance, international policy, and global cooperation. OPEC Fund Internship Overview The OPEC Fund Internship Program is a structured professional experience designed to allow students and early-career graduates to gain exposure to the operations of an international organization. Through this internship, participants will learn about the OPEC Fund’s mission of promoting sustainable development and economic growth in partner countries. The program aims to achieve several objectives: Provide a framework that allows students to gain meaningful professional experience Offer practical exposure to the work of an international financial institution Help interns understand the organization’s values, mission, and operational strategies Interns are assigned to departments based on their academic background, professional interests, and organizational needs. Internship Areas in the OPEC Fund Internship Participants in the OPEC Fund Internship may be placed in a wide range of departments across the organization. These departments include: Public sector operations Private sector operations Finance and investment Risk management Strategy and policy Internal audit Human resources Administration Communications Legal services Information technology This wide range of internship placements allows participants to explore various career paths within international development organizations. Responsibilities During the OPEC Fund Internship Interns selected for the OPEC Fund Internship will contribute to the daily activities of their assigned department. Typical responsibilities include: Conducting research and analysis Preparing written documents and reports Organizing data and project information Supporting staff members in ongoing projects Assisting with administrative and operational tasks Interns will work closely with experienced professionals and gain insights into the processes involved in international development programs. Benefits of the OPEC Fund Internship The OPEC Fund Internship provides several valuable benefits to participants. First, it offers exposure to the operations of a global development institution. Interns gain a deeper understanding of international development finance and policy. Second, the internship allows students to develop professional skills, including research, communication, and collaboration in a multicultural work environment. Third, interns build professional connections with experienced professionals and experts in development and finance. Finally, the program helps young professionals strengthen their resumes and improve their prospects for future careers in international organizations. Duration of the OPEC Fund Internship The internship program lasts between three and six months, depending on departmental needs and the intern’s availability. During this period, interns will work on projects and tasks relevant to their academic background and career interests. Eligibility for the OPEC Fund Internship The OPEC Fund Internship is open to students and recent graduates from diverse academic backgrounds. Applicants must meet the following conditions: Be between 19 and 25 years old Be enrolled in an undergraduate program and have completed at least two years of study Alternatively, applicants may be recent graduates who completed their studies within one year of the application deadline Be fluent in English Additional language skills such as Arabic, French, German, or Spanish are considered an advantage. Applicants should also demonstrate strong interest in the work of the OPEC Fund and the ability to work in an international multicultural environment. Skills Required for the OPEC Fund Internship Successful applicants should possess strong professional and interpersonal skills. Key competencies include: Collaboration and Teamwork Interns must be able to collaborate effectively with colleagues and stakeholders from different backgrounds. Effective Communication Participants should demonstrate the ability to communicate ideas clearly and professionally across different channels. Cross-Cultural Sensitivity Working in an international organization requires respect for diverse cultures and perspectives. Application Requirements for the OPEC Fund Internship Applicants must submit the following documents as part of their application: Completed online application form Curriculum Vitae (CV) Copy of university diploma or confirmation letter from their institution Most recent academic transcripts showing GPA A short motivation statement in English (150–250 words) The motivation statement should explain the applicant’s interest in the OPEC Fund and how the internship aligns with their academic and career goals. Deadline for the OPEC Fund Internship 2026 The application deadline for the OPEC Fund Internship 2026 is April 12, 2026. Interested applicants should ensure they submit all required documents before the deadline to be considered for the program. APPLY HERE Conclusion The OPEC Fund Internship 2026 provides an outstanding opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain professional experience in an international development organization. Through this program, interns will deepen their understanding of development finance, strengthen professional skills, and work alongside experts dedicated to promoting sustainable development worldwide. Click HERE to learn more about Prestigious OPEC Fund Internship 2026. For more Internship opportunities click here . The post Prestigious OPEC Fund Internship 2026 for Global Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: African Food Fellowship 2026: Fully Transform Your Leadership in Food Systems Across Kenya, Rwanda, and Zambia
summary: The African Food Fellowship 2026 is a prestigious leadership development initiative designed to empower mid-career professionals working within Africa’s food systems. This transformative program is currently recruiting 120 food systems leaders across Kenya, Rwanda, and Zambia , with 40 fellows selected per country . As Africa faces increasing challenges related to food security, climate change, nutrition, and market access, the need for innovative and collaborative leadership has never been greater. The African Food Fellowship responds to this need by equipping professionals with the knowledge, networks, and tools required to drive sustainable, inclusive, and resilient food systems. About the African Food Fellowship The African Food Fellowship is more than just a program—it is a dynamic, pan-African leadership network focused on driving systemic change in food systems. Participants become part of a growing network of over 230+ Fellows , collaborating across sectors to address pressing challenges in agriculture, food access, sustainability, and economic inclusion. The Fellowship includes participation in the Food Systems Leadership Programme (FSLP) —a 10-month structured learning experience delivered online with in-person engagements. Objectives of the Fellowship The African Food Fellowship is designed to: Develop transformational leaders within Africa’s food systems. Foster collaboration across sectors and countries . Equip fellows with practical tools to influence policy, markets, and systems . Strengthen sustainable, inclusive, and climate-resilient food systems . Support fellows in designing and implementing impactful solutions . Country-Specific Focus Areas Applicants must align their work with the impact areas defined for their country: Rwanda Access to Healthy and Nutritious Food Climate Smart Agriculture Inclusive Markets and Trade Go to the application form for Rwanda Kenya Horticulture for Inclusive Markets Blue Economy for Food Access to Finance Go to the application form for Kenya Zambia Access to Healthy and Nutritious Food Resilient Food Systems Inclusive Markets and Trade Go to the application form for Zambia Eligibility Criteria To be eligible for the African Food Fellowship: Applicants must be mid-career professionals aged 27–50 years Must be actively working within food systems or related sectors Must demonstrate leadership potential and commitment to impact Must be based in Kenya, Rwanda, or Zambia Work should align with country-specific impact areas Program Structure and Journey The Fellowship follows a structured and immersive journey: 1. Application Phase Submit your application before the deadline Receive confirmation within one week 2. Shortlisting Interviews Selected applicants invited to 90-minute group interviews 3. Offer Stage Successful candidates receive offers by June 2026 4. Programme Start Fellowship begins in September 2026 5. Learning Phase (10 Months) Delivered primarily online Includes: Weekly learning sessions Peer collaboration Assignments and field engagement Time commitment: Approximately 4 hours per week 6. In-Person Module A 3-day in-person session in January 2027 within participants’ countries 7. Certification Fellows receive certification from: Wageningen University Research Wasafiri Consulting 8. Post-Fellowship Engagement Continued participation in country and regional networks Program Benefits Participants gain access to a wide range of transformational benefits: 1. Networking Opportunities Connect with 230+ fellows across Africa Build long-term professional relationships 2. Leadership Development Participate in a 10-month structured leadership program Learn from industry experts and practitioners 3. Practical Learning Understand how food systems operate Identify systemic challenges and solutions 4. Collaboration and Action Work with peers to design and implement impactful initiatives 5. Access to Experts and Decision-Makers Engage with policymakers, researchers, and investors 6. Visibility and Influence Showcase your work across media, policy, and funding platforms Financial Information Programme Fee: USD 1,000 (payable in two installments) Enrollment Fee: USD 100 (mandatory) Scholarships A limited number of scholarships are available Applicants are encouraged to: Demonstrate financial need Seek sponsorship from their organizations Why You Should Apply Joining the African Food Fellowship positions you at the forefront of Africa’s food systems transformation. The program equips you with leadership skills, connects you with influential networks, and empowers you to create meaningful, scalable impact. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the African Food Fellowship 2026 is: 26 April 2026 (midnight EAT) Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early due to the high volume of applications expected. Click HERE to learn more about the African Food Fellowship 2026. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Do I need a degree in agriculture or food systems? No. Applicants from diverse professional backgrounds within the food system are welcome. Must I currently work in food systems? Yes, applicants should be actively engaged in food systems or related sectors. Do I need to be a specialist in the listed impact areas? Not necessarily, but your work should align with at least one focus area. Is fluency in English required? Yes, as the program is delivered in English. Must I live in the country of application? Yes, applicants must reside and work in Kenya, Rwanda, or Zambia. Does the Fellowship provide a stipend? No, but scholarships are available to support participation. How much time is required weekly? Approximately 4 hours per week . What happens after graduation? You will join an active alumni network and continue contributing to food systems transformation. Will I receive a certificate? Yes, from Wageningen University Research and Wasafiri Consulting. To discover more other Fellowship opportunities, visit OFY HERE.
